steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says robots will be helpful "as ______ astronauts to maintain the telescope can be expensive." The blank must describe the current situation in a way that explains why robots would be a useful substitute. The passage establishes that human astronauts have had to complete regular repair missions since 1990.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘straightening’: "Straightening astronauts" is not a meaningful phrase — straightening means making something physically straight, which makes no sense in this context. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘relying on’: "Relying on astronauts to maintain the telescope can be expensive" directly explains why robots are a better option: dependence on costly human missions is the problem robots would solve. Matches the passage's logic perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘reducing’: "Reducing astronauts to maintain the telescope" is not grammatically coherent as a causal explanation. It would mean cutting the number of astronauts, not describing why robots are helpful. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘forgetting about’: "Forgetting about astronauts" contradicts the passage, which says researchers hope robots will replace human missions — not that humans are forgotten. Also illogical as a reason robots are helpful. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
